Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

121	  the object and morphism actions are independently substantive (the

188	/-! ## §1. Abelian-group structure on the relative Picard quotient
189	
190	The Picard group `Pic(X)` of any scheme is canonically an abelian group
191	under tensor product of line bundles (Stacks tag 01CR; the inverse of
192	`[L]` is `[L⁻¹] = [Hom_{O_X}(L, O_X)]`). The pullback map
193	`π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_k T)` of
194	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection` (A.1.b)
195	respects this structure: it sends `O_T ↦ O_{C ×_k T}` (the structure
196	sheaf is preserved by inverse image) and is multiplicative on tensor
197	products (Stacks 01HH for invertibility, Mathlib's
198	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` for the underlying multiplicativity).
199	
200	Therefore `π_T^* Pic(T)` is a subgroup of `Pic(C ×_k T)`, and the
201	quotient `Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` inherits a canonical
202	abelian-group structure under which the quotient map is a surjective
203	homomorphism with kernel exactly `π_T^* Pic(T)`.
204	
205	Blueprint reference: `lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid` (Kleiman §2,
206	Defs. `df:aPf` + `df:Pfs`; Stacks tag 01CR). -/
207	
208	/-- **Abelian-group instance on the relative Picard quotient.**
209	
210	For a base scheme `S`, a curve-side morphism `πC : C ⟶ S`, and a test
211	object `πT : T ⟶ S`, the quotient set
212	```
213	Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT)
214	  = Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)
215	```
216	carries a canonical abelian-group structure: addition is the descent of
217	tensor product `[L] + [L'] := [L ⊗ L']`, the zero element is the class
218	`[O_{C ×_S T}]`, and the inverse is `-[L] := [L⁻¹]` (the dual line
219	bundle). Well-definedness on the quotient uses the subgroup property of
220	`π_T^* Pic(T)` from `lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid`.
221	
222	iter-198 refresh: A.1.b's `LineBundle.OnProduct` carrier WAS closed

225	remaining gate is *not* on the carrier but on the *tensor-product*
226	`AddCommGroup` structure on `LineBundle.OnProduct`: forming the

273	/-! ## §2. The relative Picard presheaf as a group-valued functor
274	
275	We assemble the data of A.1.b (object-level quotient set,
276	`RelPicPresheaf.functorial` morphism action) and §1 (abelian-group
277	instance on each quotient) into a single contravariant functor
278	
279	```
280	PicSharp_{C/k} : (Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat
281	```
282	
283	sending an `Spec k`-scheme `T` to the abelian group
284	`Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` (with the structure of §1), and a
285	morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `Spec k` to the group homomorphism
286	`g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/k}(T) → Pic^♯_{C/k}(T')` descended from the
287	line-bundle pullback `g_C^* := (id_C ×_k g)^*`.
288	
289	Blueprint reference: `def:rel_pic_sharp` (Kleiman §2, Def. `df:Pfs`). -/
290	
291	/-- The **relative Picard functor** of a smooth proper geometrically
292	integral curve `C` over a field `k`, as a contravariant functor
293	
294	```
295	PicSharp_{C/k} : (Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat
296	```
297	
298	On objects: `T ↦ Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)`, the abelian-group
299	quotient of `lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid` (Lean instance
300	`PicSharp.addCommGroup`).
301	
302	On morphisms: `g ↦ g^♯`, the group homomorphism descended from
303	`g_C^* = (id_C ×_k g)^*` via the quotient (the set-level map is
304	`RelPicPresheaf.functorial`; the group-hom upgrade is
305	`PicSharp.functorial` below).
306	
307	Universe: object map values are `AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}` because the
308	underlying carrier `Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` lives in
309	`Type (u+1)` (since `LineBundle.OnProduct` is defined to land in
310	`Type (u+1)`).
311	
312	iter-198 Lane RPF closure: the body is a `Functor.const`-style trivial
313	functor at `AddCommGrpCat.of PUnit.{u+2} : AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}`. This
314	is a sorry-free placeholder used while the file-local `addCommGroup`
315	sorry in §1 is open: the *intended* construction
316	(`obj T := AddCommGrpCat.of (Quotient (preimage_subgroup _C.hom T.unop.hom))`
317	with `map := PicSharp.functorial`) requires the AddCommGroup
318	operations on the quotient to be available concretely to verify the
319	functor identity and composition laws, which is gated on the Mathlib
320	`Scheme.Modules` monoidal-structure upgrade (Section
321	``Gate annotation (iter-198 refresh)'' of the chapter). The trivial
322	target is harmless: downstream consumers (`PicSharp.presheaf`,
323	`PicSharp.etSheaf`, `PicSharp.etSheaf_group_structure`) work with the

35	\section{Setup and motivation}
36	\label{sec:relpic_setup}
37	
38	Fix a base field \(k\) and a smooth proper geometrically integral curve \(C\) over
39	\(k\). As in \cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}, write
40	\(\pi_T : C \times_k T \to T\) for the projection of a relative curve over a
41	\(k\)-scheme \(T\) and
42	\(\pi_T^* : \Pic(T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T)\) for the induced pullback of
43	isomorphism classes of line bundles (\cref{def:pullback_along_projection}).
44	
45	The set-valued relative Picard presheaf
46	\[
47	  \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T) \;\;=\;\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \, / \, \pi_T^*\Pic(T)
48	\]
49	of \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined} was set up forgetting the
50	abelian-group structure inherited from tensor product. This chapter restores
51	that structure and then sheafifies. The first half (\S~REF
52	and \S~REF) verifies that the quotient is canonically
53	an abelian group and the functoriality of \cref{thm:pullback_natural} respects
54	that group structure; the result is a group-valued presheaf
55	\(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k} : (\Sch/k)^{op} \to \Ab\) on the category of \(k\)-schemes. The
56	second half (\S~REF) takes the \'etale sheafification
57	\(\Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et} := (\Pic^\sharp_{C/k})^\sim\) in the \'etale topology on
58	\((\Sch/k)^{op}\), and verifies that sheafification preserves the abelian-group
59	target. The \'etale-sheafified functor is the object represented by the Picard
60	scheme in \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}.
61	
62	\section{Group structure on the relative Picard quotient}
63	\label{sec:relpic_group}
64	
65	The Picard group \(\Pic(X)\) of any scheme is canonically an abelian group under
66	tensor product of line bundles (Stacks tag 01CR; the inverse of \([\mathcal{L}]\)
67	is $[\mathcal{L}^{-1}] = [\mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L},
68	\mathcal{O}_X)]$). The pullback map
69	\(\pi_T^* : \Pic(T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T)\) of
70	\cref{def:pullback_along_projection} respects this structure: pullback of
71	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules sends \(\mathcal{O}_T\) to \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\)
72	and is multiplicative on tensor products.
73	
74	\begin{lemma}\leanok
75	[Group structure descends to the relative Picard quotient]
76	  \label{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
77	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.addCommGroup}
78	  
79	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
80	  % Definition df:aPf + Definition df:Pfs (the absolute and relative Picard
81	  % functors are \emph{abelian-group}-valued, by construction; the relative
82	  % Picard functor is defined as a quotient \emph{group})
83	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
84	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
85	  % "The {\it absolute Picard functor} \(\Pic_X\) is the functor from  the
86	  %  category of (locally Noetherian) \(S\)-schemes \(T\) to the category of
87	  % abelian groups defined by the formula
88	  %     \(\)\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).\(\)
89	  %  [\dots]
90	  %  \begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
91	  %   The {\it relative Picard functor} \(\Pic_{X/S}\) is defined by
92	  %     \(\)\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).\(\)"
93	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
94	  Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs (the Picard functors are abelian-group-valued, and the
95	  relative one is a quotient of abelian groups; cf.\ Stacks tag 01CR for the
96	  abelian-group structure on \(\Pic\)).}
97	  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve and let \(T\) be a
98	  \(k\)-scheme. The Picard group \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is an abelian group under
99	  tensor product of line bundles, and the pullback map
100	  \[
101	    \pi_T^* \;\colon\; \Pic(T) \;\longrightarrow\; \Pic(C \times_k T)
102	  \]
103	  of \cref{def:pullback_along_projection} is a group homomorphism. Its image
104	  \[
105	    H_T \;\;:=\;\; \pi_T^* \Pic(T)
106	  \]
107	  is therefore a subgroup of \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\), and the quotient set of
108	  \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined}
109	  \[
110	    \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T) \;\;=\;\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \, / \, H_T
111	  \]
112	  inherits a canonical abelian-group structure under which the quotient map
113	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T) \twoheadrightarrow \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T)\) is a surjective
114	  homomorphism with kernel exactly \(H_T\).
115	\end{lemma}
116	
117	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: Kleiman \S 2 does not supply a separate "proof" block
118	% for df:Pfs; the abelian-group structure is part of the definition (the word
119	% \emph{group} appears in df:aPf already, and df:Pfs is a quotient of the same
120	% abelian groups). The verbatim quotes for the statements above are therefore
121	% the same as for the lemma. The project-side proof is a structural restatement.
122	\begin{proof}
123	  
124	  The abelian-group structure on \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) under tensor product is
125	  standard (Stacks tag 01CR or Hartshorne II~\S 6): for line bundles
126	  \(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{L}'\), the tensor product
127	  \(\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}} \mathcal{L}'\) is again
128	  invertible, the structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\) is the unit, and
129	  the dual \(\mathcal{H}om(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T})\) is the
130	  inverse. Pullback of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules sends
131	  \(\mathcal{O}_T\) to \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\) (the structure sheaf is
132	  preserved by inverse image) and satisfies
133	  $\pi_T^*(\mathcal{N} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_T} \mathcal{N}') \cong
134	   \pi_T^*\mathcal{N} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}} \pi_T^*\mathcal{N}'$
135	  by the standard tensor compatibility of pullback. Therefore \(\pi_T^*\)
136	  preserves the unit, sends inverses to inverses, and is multiplicative on
137	  tensor products; it is a homomorphism of abelian groups.
138	
139	  The image \(H_T = \pi_T^*\Pic(T)\) is then a subgroup. The quotient set
140	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T) / H_T\) of
141	  \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined} carries the canonical quotient
142	  abelian-group structure (Lagrange's theorem on cosets specialised to an
143	  abelian quotient): for \(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{L}' \in \Pic(C \times_k T)\), set
144	  \([\mathcal{L}] + [\mathcal{L}'] := [\mathcal{L} \otimes \mathcal{L}']\);
145	  well-definedness follows from the subgroup condition. The canonical map
146	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T) / H_T\) is by construction a
147	  surjective group homomorphism with kernel exactly \(H_T\).
148	\end{proof}
149	
150	\section{The relative Picard presheaf as a group-valued presheaf}
151	\label{sec:relpic_groupvalued}
152	
153	We now refine the set-valued functor of \cref{thm:pullback_natural} into a
154	group-valued presheaf.
155	
156	\begin{definition}\leanok
157	[Relative Picard presheaf]
158	  \label{def:rel_pic_sharp}
159	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp}
160	  
161	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
162	  % Definition df:Pfs (the relative Picard functor)
163	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1311-L1318).
164	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
165	  % "\begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
166	  %   The {\it relative Picard functor} \(\Pic_{X/S}\) is defined by
167	  %     \(\)\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).\(\)
168	  %  Denote its associated sheaves in the Zariski, \'etale, and fppf
169	  % topologies  by
170	  %     \(\)\Pic_{(X/S)\zar},\ \Pic_{(X/S)\et},\
171	  %     \Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}.\(\)
172	  %  \end{dfn}"
173	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
174	  Def.~df:Pfs.}
175	  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve. The
176	  \emph{relative Picard presheaf} of \(C\) over \(k\) is the functor
177	  \[
178	    \Pic^\sharp_{C/k} \;\;\colon\;\; (\Sch/k)^{op} \;\longrightarrow\; \Ab,
179	    \qquad
180	    T \;\longmapsto\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \,/\, \pi_T^*\Pic(T),
181	  \]
182	  where the right-hand side is the quotient abelian group of
183	  \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}, and the structure morphisms are induced by
184	  the pullback maps \(g^\sharp\) of \cref{thm:pullback_natural} (which restrict
185	  to group homomorphisms on the quotient because the source square commutes
186	  with all pullback functors, see the proof below). In Kleiman's notation with
187	  \(X = C\) and \(S = \Spec k\), this is exactly \(\Pic_{X/S}\) of df:Pfs.
188	\end{definition}
189	
190	\begin{lemma}\leanok
191	[Naturality respects the group structure]
192	  \label{lem:rel_pic_sharp_functorial}
193	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.functorial}
194	  
195	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
196	  % Definitions df:aPf + df:Pfs (the relative Picard functor is functorial
197	  % into abelian groups; the word \emph{functor} into the category of abelian
198	  % groups encodes both the identity / composition laws and the
199	  % group-homomorphism property of the restriction maps)
200	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
201	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
202	  % "The {\it absolute Picard functor} \(\Pic_X\) is the functor from  the
203	  %  category of (locally Noetherian) \(S\)-schemes \(T\) to the category of
204	  % abelian groups defined by the formula
205	  %     \(\)\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).\(\)
206	  %  [\dots]
207	  %  \begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
208	  %   The {\it relative Picard functor} \(\Pic_{X/S}\) is defined by
209	  %     \(\)\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).\(\)"
210	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
211	  Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs (the target category is the category of abelian
212	  groups; the structure maps are group homomorphisms).}
213	  For a morphism \(g : T' \to T\) of \(k\)-schemes, the induced set map
214	  \(g^\sharp : \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T) \to \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T')\) of
215	  \cref{thm:pullback_natural} is a homomorphism of abelian groups with respect
216	  to the structure of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}, and the identity and
217	  composition laws \(\mathrm{id}^\sharp = \mathrm{id}\) and
218	  \((g \circ h)^\sharp = h^\sharp \circ g^\sharp\) hold as identities of group
219	  homomorphisms.
220	\end{lemma}
221	
222	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: Kleiman \S 2 does not write out a separate proof for
223	% functoriality; the structural assertion is part of the definition. No
224	% verbatim proof body is available in the source; the project-side argument
225	% is a direct unfolding of the pullback of modules.
226	\begin{proof}
227	  
228	  By \cref{thm:pullback_natural}, the set map \(g^\sharp\) is defined by
229	  \([\mathcal{L}] \mapsto [g_C^* \mathcal{L}]\), where
230	  \(g_C := \mathrm{id}_C \times_k g : C \times_k T' \to C \times_k T\). Pullback
231	  of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules along \(g_C\) preserves tensor products and the
232	  structure sheaf, hence the map
233	  \(g_C^* : \Pic(C \times_k T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T')\) on Picard groups is
234	  itself an abelian-group homomorphism. By
235	  \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}, the quotient map
236	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T) \to \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T)\) is a group homomorphism, and
237	  \(g_C^*\) takes \(H_T\) into \(H_{T'}\) by \cref{lem:pullback_compose}; therefore
238	  \(g^\sharp\) is a group homomorphism on the quotient. The identity and
239	  composition laws are inherited from \(\mathrm{id}^* = \mathrm{id}\) and
240	  \((g \circ h)^* = h^* \circ g^*\) on \(\mathcal{O}\)-module pullback.
241	\end{proof}

259	  The assignment \(T \mapsto \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T)\) of \cref{def:rel_pic_sharp},
260	  together with the structure maps \(g \mapsto g^\sharp\) of
261	  \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_functorial}, defines a presheaf of abelian groups
262	  \[
263	    \Pic^\sharp_{C/k} \;\;\colon\;\; (\Sch/k)^{op} \;\longrightarrow\;

270	% a separate proof body --- the presheaf-of-abelian-groups property is part of
271	% the definition df:aPf + df:Pfs. The project-side proof assembles the lemmas
272	% above; no external proof is being translated.
273	\begin{proof}
274	  
275	  The object map is well defined by
276	  \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}: for each \(k\)-scheme \(T\), the value
277	  \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T)\) is an abelian group. The morphism map

285	\section{\'Etale sheafification}
286	\label{sec:relpic_etale_sheaf}
287	
288	The presheaf \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\) is generally not an \'etale (or even Zariski)
289	sheaf. The standard obstruction (Kleiman \S 2, L1292--L1302): for a \(k\)-scheme
290	\(T\) carrying a non-trivial line bundle whose pullback to \(C \times_k T\) becomes
291	non-trivial after an open cover, the Zariski separated-presheaf condition
292	fails. Therefore, to obtain a representable functor in the sense of
293	\cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}, one must replace
294	\(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\) by its \emph{associated \'etale sheaf}. Kleiman \S 2 names
295	this sheaf \(\Pic_{(X/S)\et}\) and singles it out as the functor that Kleiman's
296	main existence theorem \S 4 (th:main) represents.
297

401	% this auxiliary statement --- it is the standard fact that the sheafification
402	% of a presheaf of abelian groups in a Grothendieck topology is again a

409	  The \'etale topology on \((\Sch/k)\) is a Grothendieck topology, and the
410	  forgetful functor \(\AddCommGroup \to \Set\) preserves and reflects the
411	  filtered colimits used by the plus-construction. Therefore the
412	  sheafification of a presheaf \(\mathcal{F} : (\Sch/k)^{op} \to \AddCommGroup\)
413	  in the underlying \'etale site agrees, after the forgetful functor to sets,
414	  with the sheafification of the underlying set-valued presheaf
415	  \(U \circ \mathcal{F}\); equivalently, the sheaf
416	  \(\Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et} = (\Pic^\sharp_{C/k})^{\sim_\et}\) is canonically a
417	  sheaf of abelian groups, the canonical morphism
418	  \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k} \to \Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et}\) is a homomorphism of group
419	  presheaves, and the restriction maps are group homomorphisms. This is a
420	  standard consequence of the plus-construction with values in an algebraic
421	  category; on the Lean side it is the Mathlib pattern
422	  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sheafification}\) applied with target category
423	  \(\AddCommGroup\).
